_meta.title: Renderer structural-resend reconciliation (TKT-0024) — BUILD plan: create/wire split, per-node scope registry, children id-diff + prop reconcile, recursive subtree disposal, consumer-test upgrades. Design intake output only; build dispatched separately per ADR-0128.
_meta.author: design intake (agent-ui-doc-standards grammar; no component-design skill — renderer-core mechanism, not a ui-* control)
_meta.grounding: SPEC renderer-structural-resend.spec.md SPEC-R1..R5/N1..N3 under ADR-0128 (proposed, one fork — SPEC-R5 survivor reorder — awaiting Kim). Mechanics verified directly: renderer/tree.ts:76-108 (apply()'s pendingParents-only patch-in, the empirical gap), renderer/widget.ts:104-140 (makeCreateWidget's create+wire fusion), renderer/renderer.ts:302-328 (#makeHostCreateWidget's action/checks layering), renderer/list.ts (the per-item (scope,ac) pair this generalizes, ADR-0024 amendment 3), components/src/controls/select/select.ts:470-503 (ui-select's connect-time-only move-to-panel, grounding ADR-0053's retained disposition).
_meta.note: OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the renderer modules + consumer surfaces a build slice touches. INSIDE-OUT actions = what the reconciliation mechanism must DO once built. plan:true — no build source exists yet; dispatched to a2ui-builder AFTER Kim rules on ADR-0128's one fork (SPEC-R5).

nodes.label: widget.ts: makeCreateWidget splits into create() + wireProps() (LLD-C2)
nodes.accept: createWidget = create() then wireProps(); every existing renderer test (mount, list items) stays green byte-for-byte — a pure refactor checkpoint before any new behavior lands

nodes.label: renderer.ts: #makeHostCreateWidget splits into #create() + #wireNode() (LLD-C3)
nodes.accept: #createWidget = #create() then #wireNode(); #wireNode is exported into TreeDeps as rewireNode; every existing action/checks-wiring test stays green byte-for-byte

nodes.label: #nodeScopes map threaded through #mountNode/#mountChildrenInto for the static tree
nodes.accept: every static-tree node (not list-item instances) gets its own (Scope, AbortController) pair at mount, stored in #nodeScopes keyed by id; existing mount/teardown tests unaffected (inert until n7/n8 consume it)

nodes.label: apply() captures previous record before upsert; #reconcileNode dispatches on sameOrder/samePropsShallow (SPEC-R1..R3)
nodes.accept: a resend of an id already in surface.widgets is routed to #reconcileNode; a byte-identical resend disposes nothing (node scope's Scope reference unchanged, SPEC-R3); a resend of "root" never reconciles (SPEC-R4)

nodes.label: children id-diff reconcile + recursive subtree disposal (LLD-C5/C7)
nodes.accept: the ticket's own repro (mount root->group->msg, resend group with children:[msg,status]+new status node) renders status's content and leaves msg's element reference (===) unchanged; a removed child's node scope + ac are disposed and its DOM removed; a removed container hosting a live LLD-C6 dynamic list shows zero residual subscribers via inspect() (SPEC-N1 AC1); no survivor is moved/re-created on a pure reorder (SPEC-R5)

nodes.label: prop reconcile onto existing element (LLD-C6)
nodes.accept: a resend changing only props (no children delta) re-applies the complete new prop set to the SAME element (no factory.create() call, verified by reference equality); an omitted prop does not linger; a rebound {path} target re-resolves against the new path, not the old (SPEC-R2 AC1/AC2/AC3); a focused surviving sibling elsewhere in the tree keeps focus across an unrelated node's prop reconcile

nodes.label: consumer upgrades (build slices, post renderer-core green)
round-trip.test.ts (a2ui-live) gains a present-after-turn-3 assertion
nodes.accept: npm test round-trip — a new case asserts the DOM contains turn 3's new status/READY content AFTER turn 3 ingests (before turn 5's delete), closing the rendered-then-removed vs never-rendered blind spot the ticket names and the original review flagged as INFO

nodes.label: a2ui-chat turn 3/4 assertions upgrade routing-only -> visible-restructure
nodes.accept: the a2ui-chat.lld.md:153-157-flagged test upgrade lands: turn 3/4 assertions check the rendered DOM reflects the new content, not only the message-kind classification

nodes.label: kpi-panel corpus seed's restructure step renders through a real renderer
nodes.accept: examples/message-lifecycle.ts's fixture-validation test gains an assertion that replaying the seed's restructure step through createRenderer actually renders the added node in the DOM, not only that it validates 0-error

nodes.label: regression floor: full a2ui suite + both demo pages + catalog conformance green
nodes.accept: npm run check && npm test && npm run test:browser all green repo-wide; the ADR-0053 first-connect constraint is left explicitly documented as retained (not silently superseded) in the same wave

nodes.label: ADR-0128 ratification gate
nodes.accept: Kim has ruled on SPEC-R5 (survivor reorder — defer vs realize) and ADR-0128's status is accepted BEFORE n7..n9 are built — a build against a still-proposed ADR is out of sequence

actions.label: separate minting a widget from wiring its props/actions/checks, with zero behavior change
prove the split is byte-for-byte inert before any new behavior lands
give every static-tree node its own disposable scope, generalizing the list-item precedent
detect an already-mounted id's resend and gate reconciliation on an actual delta
reconcile a resent container's children by id-keyed diff, preserving survivor identity
tear down a removed subtree leak-free, recursively, including any nested dynamic list
reconcile a resent node's props onto its existing element without ever recreating it
prove a mid-arc restructure is actually visible, not merely routed
upgrade the chat demo's turn assertions to match
prove the corpus exemplar's restructure step actually renders
hold the whole repo's test/build gates green
gate the whole build behind Kim's ADR-0128 ruling on survivor reorder

edges.why: ADR-0128's SPEC-R5 ruling fixes whether reconciliation must also realize survivor reorder — building the diff dispatch ahead of ratification risks a wasted rewrite
the host wrapper's split depends on widget.ts already exposing wireProps standalone
rewireNode (the split's output) is the collaborator TreeDeps needs before a node scope has anything to call
the resend dispatcher needs a per-node scope to dispose/recreate against
the children reconcile is one of #reconcileNode's two dispatch branches
the prop reconcile is the other dispatch branch
the round-trip test's new assertion depends on children reconcile actually repainting the demo's status line
turn 4's data-only react also needs prop-path reconciliation intact (regression, not new behavior, but proven by the same test wave)
the chat demo's turn assertions upgrade on the SAME underlying fix the live demo proves first
the corpus seed's restructure step is a children-add, the same mechanism n8 builds
the regression floor gates on every consumer upgrade landing green, not just the renderer core
